title: Concept induction with lloomr
text: chunks evaluated vignette (call LLM APIs); show canonical workflow. OPENAI_API_KEY set, run -. Every step also standalone function plain data frames (distill_summarize(), cluster_texts(), synthesize_concepts(), …) — session convenience, requirement.
code: library(lloomr)  # df has one row per document, with columns "text" and "doc_id" sess <- lloom_session(df, text_col = "text", id_col = "doc_id")  # Estimate cost before spending anything lloom_estimate_gen_cost(sess)  # Distill -> cluster -> synthesize -> review; activate the best 8 concepts. # `seed` steers generation toward a topic of interest (optional); # `sample_n` generates concepts from a sample while scoring stays full. sess <- lloom_gen(sess, seed = "media trust", max_concepts = 8, sample_n = 200) sess$concepts  # Score every document against the active concepts sess <- lloom_score(sess) score_df <- lloom_results(sess)  # Time, tokens, and dollars per step summary(sess)

title: Concept induction with lloomr
text: model decision made create session (, standalone operators, via chat argument) — lloom_gen() lloom_score() deliberately take model argument; use session holds. choose provider choosing ellmer constructor: pass nothing, defaults gpt-5.4-nano (distill, score) gpt-5.2 (synthesis), require OPENAI_API_KEY. One caveat: clustering uses OpenAI embeddings default regardless chat provider (providers, like Anthropic, embeddings API). go fully non-OpenAI, supply embedding function:
code: # One model for everything — OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, or local: sess <- lloom_session(df, "text", "doc_id",   chat = ellmer::chat_openai(model = "gpt-5.2", echo = "none"))  sess <- lloom_session(df, "text", "doc_id",   chat = ellmer::chat_anthropic(model = "claude-sonnet-4-6", echo = "none"))  sess <- lloom_session(df, "text", "doc_id",   chat = ellmer::chat_ollama(model = "llama3.3", echo = "none"))  # local  # Or mix per step — cheap for high-volume distill/score, capable for # synthesis (this mirrors the package defaults): sess <- lloom_session(df, "text", "doc_id",   distill_chat = ellmer::chat_openai(model = "gpt-5.4-nano", echo = "none"),   synth_chat   = ellmer::chat_openai(model = "gpt-5.2", echo = "none"),   score_chat   = ellmer::chat_openai(model = "gpt-5.4-nano", echo = "none")) sess <- lloom_session(df, "text", "doc_id",   chat = ellmer::chat_anthropic(model = "claude-sonnet-4-6", echo = "none"),   embed_fn = function(texts) my_embedding_matrix(texts))  # any fn: texts -> matrix

what: Bring your own concepts (scoring a human codebook)
title: Concept induction with lloomr
text: Concept generation optional. already codebook — theory-driven categories, concepts previous study, hand-edited output earlier run — can use lloomr purely deductively: write concepts score every document . concept just needs name yes/inclusion-criterion question: works inside session — skip lloom_gen() entirely add concepts hand: mutually exclusive categories, [assign_topics()] accepts plain character vector topic names — concept objects needed .
code: codebook <- new_concepts(   name = c("Economic Anxiety", "Media Distrust"),   prompt = c(     "Does the text express concern about economic conditions?",     "Does the text express distrust toward news media?"   ),   active = TRUE )  chat <- ellmer::chat_openai(model = "gpt-5.4-nano", echo = "none") score_df <- score_concepts(df, "text", "doc_id", codebook, chat,                            get_highlights = TRUE) sess <- lloom_session(df, "text", "doc_id") sess <- lloom_add_concept(sess, "Economic Anxiety",                           "Does the text express concern about economic conditions?") sess <- lloom_add_concept(sess, "Media Distrust",                           "Does the text express distrust toward news media?") sess <- lloom_score(sess)

title: Concept induction with lloomr
text: Scoring multi-label: (document, concept) pair rated independently, document can match several concepts none. mutually exclusive partition needed, freeze topic set use one :
code: # Forced-choice LLM classification (labels constrained to your topic set) assignments <- assign_topics(df, "text", "doc_id", sess$concepts, chat)  # Or: free, deterministic argmax over existing scores slotted <- slot_by_score(score_df, "doc_id", threshold = 1)

title: Concept induction with lloomr
text: [lloom_vis()] draws concept--group heatmap (R replacement Python package’s interactive matrix widget). needs scored session, demonstrate small synthetic one — API calls:  underlying tidy matrix available via [concept_matrix()] custom plots, [lloom_export()] produces per-concept evidence table (criteria, prevalence, highlight quotes).
code: docs <- data.frame(   doc_id = as.character(1:8),   text = paste("document", 1:8),   party = rep(c("D", "R"), each = 4) ) concepts <- new_concepts(   name = c("Media Distrust", "Vaccine Promotion"),   prompt = c("Distrusts media?", "Promotes vaccines?"),   active = TRUE )  # A synthetic score grid (in practice: lloom_score() / score_concepts()) score_df <- expand.grid(   doc_id = docs$doc_id, concept_name = concepts$name,   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) score_df$concept_id <- concepts$id[match(score_df$concept_name, concepts$name)] score_df$text <- docs$text[match(score_df$doc_id, docs$doc_id)] score_df$score <- c(1, 1, 0, 0, 1, 1, 1, 0,   # Media Distrust                     0, 1, 1, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0)   # Vaccine Promotion score_df$rationale <- "" score_df$highlight <- "" score_df$concept_seed <- NA_character_  sess <- lloom_session(docs, "text", "doc_id",                       distill_chat = "unused", synth_chat = "unused",                       score_chat = "unused") sess$concepts <- concepts sess$score_df <- score_df lloom_vis(sess, slice_col = "party")

title: Concept induction with lloomr
text: score table plain data frame — one row per (document, concept) pair — saving one ordinary line: CSV everything: document IDs, concept names, scores, model’s rationales, highlight quotes. analysis usually also want wide version — one row per document, one column per concept — join onto main dataset. [scores_wide()] pivot sanitizes concept names valid column names (continuing synthetic scores ): Joining scores back onto data regular merge — row-count checks merge deserves: Finally, two one-liners whole-analysis persistence:
code: readr::write_csv(lloom_results(sess), "scores.csv") wide <- scores_wide(score_df, "doc_id") wide #> # A tibble: 8 × 3 #>   doc_id Media.Distrust Vaccine.Promotion #>   <chr>           <dbl>             <dbl> #> 1 1                   1                 0 #> 2 2                   1                 1 #> 3 3                   0                 1 #> 4 4                   0                 0 #> 5 5                   1                 0 #> 6 6                   1                 0 #> 7 7                   1                 0 #> 8 8                   0                 0  # The mapping from concept names to column names: attr(wide, "concept_names") #>      Media Distrust   Vaccine Promotion  #>    "Media.Distrust" "Vaccine.Promotion" stopifnot(nrow(wide) == nrow(docs)) merged <- dplyr::left_join(docs, wide, by = "doc_id") stopifnot(nrow(merged) == nrow(docs)) head(merged, 3) #>   doc_id       text party Media.Distrust Vaccine.Promotion #> 1      1 document 1     D              1                 0 #> 2      2 document 2     D              1                 1 #> 3      3 document 3     D              0                 1 # Everything at once: scores (long + wide), concepts, evidence table, # and the full session object lloom_write(sess, "results/")  # Or just the session; chat objects survive and keep working after reload saveRDS(sess, "results/session.rds") sess <- readRDS("results/session.rds")   # later, in a fresh R session

what: lloomr: Concept Induction from Text with LLMs
title: lloomr: Concept Induction from Text with LLMs
text: R implementation LLooM concept-induction algorithm (Lam et al., 2024, CHI 2024; Python package text_lloom). collection texts, lloomr induces interpretable concepts — short name plus one-sentence inclusion criterion — scores every document every concept: LLM calls go ellmer, supported provider works (OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, local models), responses constrained structured-output schemas rather parsed free text.
code: distill (quotes → bullets) → cluster (embeddings + UMAP + HDBSCAN)   → synthesize (LLM proposes concepts) → review (remove/merge/select)   → score (every document × every concept) → refine / loop

title: lloomr: Concept Induction from Text with LLMs
text: Set OPENAI_API_KEY ~/.Renviron default models — pick provider ; model choice just ellmer chat object:
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("zilinskyjan/lloomr")  # Alternative:  # remotes::install_github("zilinskyjan/lloomr") sess <- lloom_session(df, "text", "doc_id",   chat = ellmer::chat_anthropic(model = "claude-sonnet-4-6", echo = "none"))

title: lloomr: Concept Induction from Text with LLMs
text: Every pipeline step also standalone function plain data frames (distill_summarize(), cluster_texts(), synthesize_concepts(), review_concepts(), score_concepts(), …). Beyond Python original, lloomr adds single-label classification fixed topic set: assign_topics() (forced-choice, schema- constrained) slot_by_score() (free argmax existing scores). Concept generation optional. score human-written codebook, skip generation entirely:
code: library(lloomr)  sess <- lloom_session(df, text_col = "text", id_col = "doc_id") lloom_estimate_gen_cost(sess)                  # pre-flight cost estimate sess <- lloom_gen(sess, max_concepts = 8)      # induce + select concepts sess <- lloom_score(sess)                      # score all documents lloom_results(sess)                            # tidy (doc × concept) scores lloom_vis(sess, slice_col = "party")           # heatmap by group summary(sess)                                  # time, tokens, dollars  # Save your results — the score table is a plain data frame: readr::write_csv(lloom_results(sess), "scores.csv") lloom_write(sess, "results/")                  # or: everything in one call codebook <- new_concepts(   name   = c("Economic Anxiety", "Media Distrust"),   prompt = c("Does the text express concern about economic conditions?",              "Does the text express distrust toward news media?"),   active = TRUE ) score_df <- score_concepts(df, "text", "doc_id", codebook, chat)

title: lloomr: Concept Induction from Text with LLMs
text: vignette("lloomr") — package vignette (pipeline, single-label classification, visualization) Function reference: every operator documented examples (?lloom_gen, ?score_concepts, …) Default session models: gpt-5.4-nano cheap high-volume steps (distill, score), gpt-5.2 concept synthesis; override distill_chat / synth_chat / score_chat

what: Assign each document to exactly one topic — assign_topics
title: Assign each document to exactly one topic — assign_topics
text: Forced-choice classification documents fixed topic set (typically concepts kept review_concepts() / refine_concepts(), possibly edited user). Unlike score_concepts(), rates every (document, concept) pair independently (multi-label), returns exactly one topic per document. topic label constrained provided set via structured-output schema, model invent labels.

title: Assign each document to exactly one topic — assign_topics
code: assign_topics(   df,   text_col,   id_col,   topics,   chat,   allow_other = TRUE,   other_label = "Other",   batch_size = 5,   max_active = 10,   rpm = 500 )

title: Assign each document to exactly one topic — assign_topics
text: df Data frame documents. text_col, id_col Column names (strings) document text IDs. topics fixed topic set: either concept tibble (names prompts shown model) character vector topic names. chat ellmer chat object. allow_other TRUE (default), adds other_label option documents fitting none topics; FALSE model must force every document one topics. other_label Label used non-fitting documents. Default "". batch_size Documents per LLM call. Default 5. max_active, rpm Concurrency controls passed ll_query().

title: Assign each document to exactly one topic — assign_topics
text: tibble one row per document: id_col, text, topic, rationale. Documents missing LLM responses get topic = NA (warning). Token/cost usage attached attribute "usage".

title: Assign each document to exactly one topic — assign_topics
text: lloomr extension; upstream Python package single-label operator.

title: Assign each document to exactly one topic — assign_topics
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ topics <- new_concepts(   name = c("Vaccine Promotion", "Media Distrust"),   prompt = c("Promotes vaccination?", "Expresses distrust of media?") ) assignments <- assign_topics(df, "text", "post_id", topics, chat) table(assignments$topic) } # }

what: Cluster texts by semantic similarity — cluster_texts
title: Cluster texts by semantic similarity — cluster_texts
text: Embeds text (typically bullets distill_summarize()), reduces embeddings 5 dimensions UMAP, clusters HDBSCAN. cluster later becomes one batch examples concept synthesis.

title: Cluster texts by semantic similarity — cluster_texts
code: cluster_texts(   df,   text_col,   id_col,   embed_fn = NULL,   embed_model = "text-embedding-3-large",   embeddings = NULL,   min_cluster_size = NULL,   randomize = FALSE,   batch_size = 20,   seed = NULL )

title: Cluster texts by semantic similarity — cluster_texts
text: df Data frame texts (typically one row per bullet). text_col, id_col Column names (strings) text document IDs. embed_fn Function mapping character vector embedding matrix (one row per text). Defaults ll_embed() embed_model. Supply use different provider. embed_model Embedding model name passed ll_embed() embed_fn supplied. Default "text-embedding-3-large" (upstream default). embeddings Optional precomputed embedding matrix (one row per row df empty-text filtering); skips embedding call. min_cluster_size Minimum HDBSCAN cluster size. Default NULL = max(3, floor(n/10)) (upstream heuristic). randomize TRUE, skip embeddings entirely assign shuffled texts pseudo-clusters batch_size (upstream's randomize mode, ablation/fallback). batch_size Pseudo-cluster size randomize mode. Default 20. seed Optional integer; supplied, clustering reproducible (sets RNG seed runs UMAP single-threaded).

title: Cluster texts by semantic similarity — cluster_texts
text: tibble columns id_col, text_col, cluster_id (integer; -1 = noise/outliers, clusters numbered 0), sorted cluster_id. embedding matrix attached attribute "embeddings" (NULL randomize mode).

title: Cluster texts by semantic similarity — cluster_texts
code: df <- data.frame(id = 1:6, text = paste("bullet point", 1:6))  # randomize mode needs no embeddings (upstream's ablation/fallback) cluster_texts(df, "text", "id", randomize = TRUE, batch_size = 3, seed = 1) #> # A tibble: 6 × 3 #>   id    text           cluster_id #>   <chr> <chr>               <dbl> #> 1 1     bullet point 1          0 #> 2 3     bullet point 3          0 #> 3 4     bullet point 4          0 #> 4 2     bullet point 2          1 #> 5 5     bullet point 5          1 #> 6 6     bullet point 6          1  if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Real clustering: embeds via the OpenAI API, then UMAP + HDBSCAN cluster_texts(bullets, "text", "post_id", seed = 42) } # }

what: Build the concept x slice match matrix — concept_matrix
title: Build the concept x slice match matrix — concept_matrix
text: tidy data behind lloom_vis() (replaces upstream prep_vis_dfs()): every group documents (slice metadata column, plus "") every concept, number matching documents. synthetic "Outlier" concept counts documents matching concept (upstream).

title: Build the concept x slice match matrix — concept_matrix
code: concept_matrix(   score_df,   id_col,   slice_df = NULL,   slice_col = NULL,   threshold = 1,   norm_by = c("none", "slice", "concept"),   max_slice_bins = 5,   include_outlier = TRUE )

title: Build the concept x slice match matrix — concept_matrix
text: score_df Output score_concepts(). id_col Column name document IDs. slice_df Optional data frame id_col slice_col, supplying metadata column slice (typically original input data). slice_col Optional column slice_df group documents . Character/factor columns use values; numeric columns binned max_slice_bins quantile bins. threshold Minimum score counting match. Default 1. norm_by "none" (raw counts), "slice" (fraction slice's documents), "concept" (fraction concept's total matches). max_slice_bins Maximum bins numeric slice columns. Default 5. include_outlier Include "Outlier" row. Default TRUE.

title: Build the concept x slice match matrix — concept_matrix
text: tibble: slice, concept, n (match count), value (normalized per norm_by), slice_size (documents slice).

title: Build the concept x slice match matrix — concept_matrix
code: score_df <- data.frame(   doc_id = rep(c("1", "2", "3", "4"), each = 2),   concept_name = rep(c("Economy", "Media"), 4),   score = c(1, 0,  1, 1,  0, 1,  0, 0) ) meta <- data.frame(doc_id = as.character(1:4), party = c("D", "D", "R", "R")) concept_matrix(score_df, "doc_id", slice_df = meta, slice_col = "party",                norm_by = "slice") #> # A tibble: 9 × 5 #>   slice concept     n slice_size value #>   <chr> <chr>   <int>      <int> <dbl> #> 1 All   Economy     2          4  0.5  #> 2 All   Media       2          4  0.5  #> 3 All   Outlier     1          4  0.25 #> 4 D     Economy     2          2  1    #> 5 D     Media       1          2  0.5  #> 6 D     Outlier     0          2  0    #> 7 R     Economy     0          2  0    #> 8 R     Media       1          2  0.5  #> 9 R     Outlier     1          2  0.5

what: Distill documents to salient quotes — distill_filter
title: Distill documents to salient quotes — distill_filter

Default 3. seed Optional seed term steer extraction (e.g. "media distrust"). prompt_template Optional custom template; must contain fields required validate_prompt("distill_filter", ...). max_active, rpm Concurrency controls passed ll_query().

what: Run many structured LLM queries concurrently — ll_query
title: Run many structured LLM queries concurrently — ll_query
text: workhorse behind every LLooM operator (replaces upstream multi_query_gpt_wrapper()). Sends prompts concurrently ellmer::parallel_chat_structured() given output schema returns one parsed result per prompt. Failed queries yield NULL (warning) rather aborting batch, mirroring upstream behavior.

title: Run many structured LLM queries concurrently — ll_query
code: ll_query(chat, prompts, type, max_active = 10, rpm = 500)

title: Run many structured LLM queries concurrently — ll_query
text: chat ellmer chat object, e.g. ellmer::chat_openai(). prompts Character vector list prompt strings. type ellmer type spec describing response shape (see lloom_type()). max_active Maximum number simultaneous requests. Default 10. rpm Requests-per-minute throttle. Default 500.

title: Run many structured LLM queries concurrently — ll_query
text: list one element per prompt: parsed result (list matching type), NULL query failed. Token/cost usage call attached attribute "usage" (data frame, NULL unavailable).

title: Run many structured LLM queries concurrently — ll_query
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ chat <- ellmer::chat_openai(model = "gpt-5.4-nano", echo = "none") prompts <- sapply(c("The economy is improving.", "Media trust is falling."),   function(ex) render_prompt(     lloom_prompt("distill_summarize"),     list(ex = ex, seeding_phrase = "", n_bullets = 2, n_words = "3-5")   )) res <- ll_query(chat, prompts, lloom_type("distill_summarize")) str(res) attr(res, "usage")  # tokens and dollars } # }

title: Create a LLooM session — lloom_session
text: df Data frame one row per document. text_col Name text column. id_col Name document ID column; NULL, id column row numbers created (message). IDs must unique. chat Optional: single ellmer chat object use LLM steps. model provider chosen — ellmer provider works (ellmer::chat_openai(), ellmer::chat_anthropic(), ellmer::chat_google_gemini(), ellmer::chat_ollama(), ...). Overridden step-specific arguments . distill_chat, synth_chat, score_chat ellmer chat objects distill, synthesize/review, score steps individually (common pattern: cheap model high-volume distill/score steps, capable one synthesis). Defaults neither chat given: gpt-5.4-nano distill/score gpt-5.2 synthesis (requires OPENAI_API_KEY). Upstream's defaults gpt-4o-mini / gpt-4o; lloomr tracks newer models (deviation D10 comparison document). embed_fn Embedding function clustering (see cluster_texts()). Default: ll_embed() embed_model. embed_model Embedding model default embed_fn.

what: lloomr: Concept Induction from Text with Large Language Models — lloomr-package
title: lloomr: Concept Induction from Text with Large Language Models — lloomr-package
text: R implementation LLooM concept induction algorithm (Lam et al., 2024, doi:10.1145/3613904.3642830 ) extracting applying interpretable, high-level concepts text data using large language models. pipeline distills documents salient excerpts bullet-point summaries, clusters via text embeddings, asks LLM synthesize named concepts inclusion criteria, reviews deduplicates concept set, scores every document every concept. Translated Python package 'text_lloom'.

title: Score documents against concepts — score_concepts
text: deductive step LLooM: every document rated every concept's inclusion criterion 5-point scale ("strongly agree" ... E "strongly disagree", mapped 1, 0.75, 0.5, 0.25, 0 letter_to_score()). Documents scored batches per concept, concept-batches run concurrently single ll_query() call.

title: Score documents against concepts — score_concepts
text: df Data frame documents score (typically full dataset, even concepts generated sample). text_col, id_col Column names (strings) document text IDs. concepts Concept tibble (see new_concepts()). rows scored; filter active concepts first want (session pipeline automatically). chat ellmer chat object (high-volume step; cheap model like gpt-4o-mini upstream default). batch_size Documents per LLM call. Default 5 (upstream default; upstream's session pipeline uses 1). get_highlights TRUE, also ask supporting quote example (stored highlight). Default FALSE. max_active, rpm Concurrency controls passed ll_query().

text: Every (document, concept) pair guaranteed appear exactly output: pairs missing LLM responses (failed queries, skipped IDs) backfilled score 0 empty rationale, upstream.

title: Reshape scores to one row per document (wide matrix) — scores_wide
code: score_df <- data.frame(   doc_id = rep(c("1", "2"), each = 2),   text = rep(c("first doc", "second doc"), each = 2),   concept_name = rep(c("Media Distrust", "Vaccine Promotion"), 2),   score = c(1, 0, 0.25, 1) ) wide <- scores_wide(score_df, "doc_id") wide #> # A tibble: 2 × 3 #>   doc_id Media.Distrust Vaccine.Promotion #>   <chr>           <dbl>             <dbl> #> 1 1                1                    0 #> 2 2                0.25                 1 attr(wide, "concept_names") #>      Media Distrust   Vaccine Promotion  #>    "Media.Distrust" "Vaccine.Promotion"   # Join back onto a main dataset, with the safety checks lloomr # recommends for any merge: main <- data.frame(doc_id = c("1", "2"), party = c("D", "R")) stopifnot(nrow(wide) == nrow(main)) merged <- merge(main, wide, by = "doc_id") stopifnot(nrow(merged) == nrow(main)) merged #>   doc_id party Media.Distrust Vaccine.Promotion #> 1      1     D           1.00                 0 #> 2      2     R           0.25                 1

what: Slot each document into one topic from existing scores — slot_by_score
title: Slot each document into one topic from existing scores — slot_by_score
text: Deterministic single-label assignment derived multi-label score_concepts() result: document gets concept scored highest. Costs LLM calls. Ties non-matches handled explicitly: document's best score threshold, labeled other_label; several concepts tie best score, first score_df order wins tie = TRUE flags ambiguity.

title: Slot each document into one topic from existing scores — slot_by_score
text: score_df Output score_concepts(). id_col Column name document IDs. threshold Minimum best score required assign topic; document labeled other_label. Default 0.75 ("agree" stronger). other_label Label documents matching concept. Default "".

title: Slot each document into one topic from existing scores — slot_by_score
text: tibble one row per document: id_col, text, topic, topic_score (winning score), tie (logical).

title: Slot each document into one topic from existing scores — slot_by_score
code: score_df <- data.frame(   doc_id = rep(c("1", "2", "3"), each = 2),   text = rep(c("clear economy doc", "ambiguous doc", "off-topic doc"), each = 2),   concept_name = rep(c("Economy", "Media"), 3),   score = c(1, 0.25,   1, 1,   0.5, 0.25) ) # Doc 1: Economy wins; doc 2: tie (flagged); doc 3: below threshold -> Other slot_by_score(score_df, "doc_id") #> # A tibble: 3 × 5 #>   doc_id text              topic   topic_score tie   #>   <chr>  <chr>             <chr>         <dbl> <lgl> #> 1 1      clear economy doc Economy         1   FALSE #> 2 2      ambiguous doc     Economy         1   TRUE  #> 3 3      off-topic doc     Other           0.5 FALSE

title: lloomr (development version)
text: Printing concept table dplyr column subsetting (e.g. sess$concepts |> select(name)) longer warns unknown active column falsely reports “0 active”. <lloom_concepts> header now shown full concept structure present.

title: lloomr (development version)
text: lloom_session() gains chat argument: one ellmer chat object (provider) configures three pipeline steps; step-specific arguments still override . review_remove() refuses remove concepts (keeps warns guidance), auto-review can longer leave lloom_gen() empty concept set. New results-persistence helpers: scores_wide() (document x concept matrix sanitized column names join-safety assertions) lloom_write() (one call writes long/wide scores, concept table, evidence table, session.rds). lloom_export(collapse = TRUE) flattens list columns evidence table writes straight CSV. Documentation: explicit saving guidance (readr::write_csv(...)) manual, vignette, README; new vignette section scoring human-written codebook without running concept generation.

title: lloomr 0.1.0
text: First release. Complete R implementation LLooM concept-induction algorithm (Lam et al., 2024, CHI), translated Python package text_lloom (upstream commit 8252533).

title: lloomr 0.1.0
text: Distill operators: distill_filter(), distill_summarize(). Clustering: cluster_texts() (embeddings + UMAP + HDBSCAN), reproducibility seed pluggable embed_fn. Concept synthesis review: synthesize_concepts(), review_remove(), review_merge(), review_select(), review_concepts(). Scoring refinement: score_concepts(), summarize_concept(), refine_concepts(), loop_docs(). Session pipeline: lloom_session(), lloom_gen(), lloom_score(), lloom_gen_auto(), cost estimators, summary()/print() methods. Visualization export: concept_matrix(), lloom_vis(), lloom_export().

title: lloomr 0.1.0
text: Single-label classification fixed topic set: assign_topics() (forced-choice, schema-constrained) slot_by_score() (argmax existing scores). Provider-agnostic LLM layer via ellmer structured output (typed schemas instead JSON--text parsing); works OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, local models. lloom_gen(sample_n = ): generate concepts sample scoring full dataset. Token/cost usage attached every LLM-calling result. Four upstream bugs fixed (batched-synthesis misattribution, merge-guard -op ID overwrite, dedupe inconsistency, silent score backfill) — see project’s comparison document details. Default session models: gpt-5.4-nano (distill, score) gpt-5.2 (synthesis); ellmer chat object can substituted per step.
